example: We had to bite the bullet and tell the client about the delay.

meaning: быть на шаг впереди в развитии или знаниях
example: Our team stays ahead of the curve by learning new tools early.
Someone dropped the ball and the report wasn't sent on time.
hint: не справиться с задачей в нужный момент

example: The burning question is: how do we cut costs without losing quality?

meaning: не принять чью-либо сторону, быть в нерешительности
example: She's still on the fence about the new strategy.

meaning: менять условия или требования в процессе работы
example: The client kept moving the goalposts, so the deadline slipped.
hint: менять правила игры в последний момент

example: Who is going to take the lead on the client presentation?
Please keep me in the loop about any changes.

meaning: перекладывать задачу или вину на другого человека
example: Don't pass the buck — own your mistakes and fix them.
hint: свалить вину или задачу на кого-то другого

meaning: сосредоточиться на простых и фундаментальных вещах
example: When the new method failed, we decided to go back to basics.
After months of delays, the team threw in the towel on the feature.

meaning: ситуация без чётких правил или однозначного ответа
example: The contract terms are a grey area — we need legal advice.

meaning: сделать что-то в самый последний момент
example: They submitted the proposal at the eleventh hour.
When the manager left, she stepped up to the plate and led the team.
The pilot results look good — we're on the right track.

meaning: попытка, у которой мало шансов на успех
example: Winning that contract is a long shot, but worth trying.
Let's have a quick meeting to clear the air after yesterday.
We finally got the green light to launch the campaign.
I'll take your feedback on board and revise the draft.

idiom: bite off more than you can chew
meaning: взять на себя больше, чем реально можешь сделать
example: He bit off more than he could chew by managing three projects at once.
Our compliance team always does everything by the book.

meaning: иметь мало денег для работы или проекта
example: We delivered a great result even on a tight budget.
The merger plans are still up in the air.
After the project failed, he had to face the music with the board.
The bottom line is: we need to reduce costs by 20%.
She always goes the extra mile for her clients.

meaning: попытка без достаточной информации или знаний
example: Guessing the budget without data is a shot in the dark.
He pulled some strings to get the meeting with the director.
The small team worked under the radar and launched quietly.

meaning: говорить уверенно, но не обязательно подтверждать делом
example: He talks the talk in meetings, but rarely delivers results.
